name: CoalescingActuator: 51 dispatches for same service → ≤2 executions
aim: 51 rapid dispatches for svcID="svc-coalesce" must result in ≤2 actual backend executions (map coalescing)

threshold.rationale: pending map keyed by serviceID must overwrite — N dispatches = 1 execution under rapid fire

result.fault_injected: 51 rapid dispatches for same svcID with no sleep

on_exceed: CoalescingActuator sends every directive to backend — backend overwhelmed under burst dispatch

answered_questions.what_fault_was_injected: 51 rapid dispatches for same serviceID with no yield between them
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: pending map keyed by serviceID must overwrite — this is the stated contract of CoalescingActuator
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Backend overwhelmed with 50+ requests per control tick → backend latency spikes → actuator timeout cascade
answered_questions.how_fault_was_injected: Rapid-fire dispatches with no sleep; pending map should accumulate into one entry
answered_questions.what_degraded_mode_means: Only latest ScaleFactor is executed; intermediate values discarded (by design)

name: CoalescingActuator.Close() drains all pending before returning
aim: Dispatch 5 services then Close — all 5 must be executed before Close returns

threshold.rationale: Pending directives must not be lost on graceful shutdown

on_exceed: Pending directives silently lost on shutdown — last control action before restart never applied

answered_questions.why_this_threshold: All pending must be flushed — graceful shutdown contract
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: On Kubernetes restart, last directive not applied — system state doesn't match controller's model
answered_questions.how_fault_was_injected: Immediate Close() after single Dispatch — races with background execution loop
answered_questions.how_recovery_was_verified: QueueBackend.WorkerCount verifies each service's directive was actually executed
answered_questions.what_degraded_mode_means: N/A — this is the normal shutdown path

name: QueueBackend worker count correct through scale-up/down sequence
aim: scale sequence [3.0, 2.0, 0.5, 0.3] applied to svcID produces correct worker counts; floor at 1

threshold.rationale: QueueBackend must handle all valid scale factors without panic

result.fault_injected: scale sequence with sub-1.0 scale factors that floor at minWorkers=1

on_exceed: QueueBackend produces wrong worker count → scale decisions do not match what backend applies

answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Worker count must match math.Round(current × scaleFactor) with floor at minWorkers
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Control loop believes it scaled service down but backend has wrong worker count
answered_questions.how_fault_was_injected: Sub-floor scale factor (0.3 × 3 = 0.9 → must floor at 1)
answered_questions.what_degraded_mode_means: N/A — this tests the in-memory mock backend, not a degraded path

name: persistence.NewWriter nil-safe when DB unavailable
aim: NewWriter(unreachable DB) returns nil; Enqueue(nil) and Close(nil) do not panic

threshold.rationale: Orchestrator runs without DB — all persistence calls must be nil-safe

result.fault_injected: NewWriter with DSN pointing to port 1 (always connection refused)

on_exceed: NewWriter panics on DB unavailable → orchestrator startup crashes → system never starts

answered_questions.what_fault_was_injected: DSN with port 1 (connection refused) passed to NewWriter
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Zero panics: DATABASE_URL may be unset in deployment; system must start without it
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Orchestrator panics at startup when DATABASE_URL points to unavailable DB → pod crash loop
answered_questions.how_fault_was_injected: persistence.NewWriter("postgres://...localhost:1/nonexistentdb", 10)
answered_questions.what_degraded_mode_means: Writer is nil; all Enqueue/Close calls are no-ops; snapshots not persisted (acceptable)

name: Writer.Enqueue non-blocking: 10000 calls on nil writer < 10ms
aim: 10000 Enqueue calls on nil Writer must complete in < 10ms total (no blocking)

threshold.rationale: Enqueue is called every orchestrator tick — must be instant even when queue is full

on_exceed: Enqueue blocks → orchestrator tick stage 9 (persistence) blocks → tick overrun → safety mode

answered_questions.what_fault_was_injected: nil Writer (simulates DB unavailable or queue full drop path)
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 10ms for 10k calls = 1µs each — the nil guard must be a single branch check
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Persistence blocking orchestrator tick → tick deadline exceeded → stage skipping → data loss
answered_questions.how_fault_was_injected: var w *persistence.Writer = nil; w.Enqueue(...) calls nil-safe guard
answered_questions.what_degraded_mode_means: nil Writer drops all snapshots silently — system runs without DB persistence

name: Backend +500ms latency: non-blocking Dispatch + coalescing + recovery
aim: Dispatch<5ms; coalescing>=2.0:1; recovery<100ms

threshold.rationale: Dispatch must never block the orchestrator tick loop

error_messages: max_disp=0.000ms dispatched=20 http=1 ratio=20.00 rec_ms=0 rec_ok=false

on_exceed: CoalescingActuator blocks orchestrator tick → tick deadline exceeded → safety mode

answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Dispatch<5ms: non-blocking is the core contract of CoalescingActuator
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Tick loop blocked → consecutive overruns → safety level escalation
answered_questions.how_fault_was_injected: atomic int32 controlling time.Sleep in handler; cleared for recovery phase
answered_questions.what_degraded_mode_means: pending map coalesces by serviceID — latest directive wins; caller never blocks

name: Backend disconnection: graceful failure propagation
aim: All 10 services get ActuationResult{Success:false}, 0 panics, goroutine_leak<=3

threshold.rationale: Connection error must be caught as error return, never re-panicked

on_exceed: Actuator goroutine panics on connection error → control loop goroutine crashes → open-loop

answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Zero panics: network errors are expected in production
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: CoalescingActuator goroutine panics → feedback channel never drained → orchestrator blocked
answered_questions.how_fault_was_injected: server closed before HTTPBackend.Execute makes first TCP connection
answered_questions.how_recovery_was_verified: Not tested here — see L6-NET-001 for recovery
answered_questions.what_degraded_mode_means: All ActuationResult.Success=false with Error; orchestrator logs and continues

name: RouterBackend partial failure isolation
aim: svc-a (5xx) fails 100%; svc-b (200) succeeds 100%; zero cross-contamination

threshold.rationale: A broken route for one service must not affect unrelated services

error_messages: svc-a: ok=0 fail=5 | svc-b: ok=5 fail=0 | b_rate=1.000 a_fail=1.000

on_exceed: One broken route contaminates all routes — cascading failure instead of isolation

answered_questions.what_fault_was_injected: svc-a AddRoute'd to HTTP 500 server; svc-b AddRoute'd to HTTP 200 server
answered_questions.why_this_threshold: svc-b must be 100% unaffected — route isolation is the contract of RouterBackend
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: A single degraded service's backend degrades control for ALL services
answered_questions.how_fault_was_injected: router.AddRoute with independent broken and working backends
answered_questions.what_degraded_mode_means: svc-a commands fail with error; svc-b commands succeed normally

name: Backend HTTP 503: ActuationResult.Error contains status code
aim: HTTP 503 → ActuationResult{Success:false, Error contains '503'}; zero panics

threshold.rationale: 5xx is expected in production — error, not panic

error_messages: panics=0 success=false err=http backend: status=503 has_503=true

on_exceed: HTTPBackend panics on non-2xx → actuator goroutine crashes → actuator stops

answered_questions.why_this_threshold: 503 is most common production backend error — must produce typed error
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Backend 5xx causes panic → process crash → Kubernetes restart
answered_questions.how_fault_was_injected: handler: w.WriteHeader(http.StatusServiceUnavailable)
answered_questions.what_degraded_mode_means: ActuationResult.Error = 'http backend: status=503'; orchestrator logs and continues

name: QueueBackend worker count correct through scale-up/down sequence
aim: scale sequence [3.0, 2.0, 0.5, 0.3] applied to svcID produces correct worker counts; floor at 1

threshold.rationale: QueueBackend must handle all valid scale factors without panic

result.fault_injected: scale sequence with sub-1.0 scale factors that floor at minWorkers=1

on_exceed: QueueBackend produces wrong worker count → scale decisions do not match what backend applies

answered_questions.why_this_threshold: Worker count must match math.Round(current × scaleFactor) with floor at minWorkers
answered_questions.what_happens_if_it_fails: Control loop believes it scaled service down but backend has wrong worker count
answered_questions.how_fault_was_injected: Sub-floor scale factor (0.3 × 3 = 0.9 → must floor at 1)
answered_questions.what_degraded_mode_means: N/A — this tests the in-memory mock backend, not a degraded path
